summary: F1 and F2 produce AttestedRoots over their respective blocklace tips; both federations cross-register descriptors; tampered descriptors are rejected at registration; federation_ids are committee-derived (audit F1).

must_not_pass_explanation.tampered_federation_descriptor_rejected: Append a fresh validator to F1's descriptor while keeping the declared federation_id. The register-federation command at node/src/main.rs:884 must recompute H(sorted_pubkeys || epoch) and refuse to write the descriptor when the recomputed id mismatches the declared one. The scenario records this assertion `true` when the CLI's exit code is non-zero (rejection occurred).

blocked_on: Lane D: FederationReceipt::with_threshold_qc lift on commit (AUDIT-protocol-composition.md seam 6) — the /federation/roots endpoint exposes the AttestedRoot but not the FederationReceipt today.
AttestedRoot's blocklace_finality field (AUDIT-federation.md F3) — when this lands, expand must_pass to include 'attested_root_carries_blocklace_finality'.
documented_gaps: Solo federation_mode runs with threshold=1; the n>=2 must_pass for committee_threshold is in a sibling scenario.
The descriptor exchange is bilateral by file copy at startup; production cross-federation discovery is out of scope.
improve_dont_degrade: When lane D lands, add to must_pass: attested_root_signature_verifies_against_committee, attested_root_carries_blocklace_finality, attested_root_federation_id_binding_holds. Add to must_not_pass: equivocating_federation_two_roots_same_height_undetected, federation_receipt_with_tampered_id_accepted, stale_committee_epoch_receipt_accepted_after_rotation.
